description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body and a narrow base, featuring two prominent, upwardly curling handles on either side. The surface is adorned with intricate geometric patterns and faded, dark brown motifs, including figures and meanders, reminiscent of Greek or Mediterranean pottery. The vase has a weathered, matte finish with visible signs of age, such as surface discoloration and minor abrasions. The handles are thick and robust, spiraling outward in a decorative flourish. The rim is broad and slightly flared, with the interior appearing hollow and spacious. This vase would serve as a striking decorative piece in a public or garden setting, evoking a sense of classical antiquity.
description: A large, weathered ceramic vase with ornate spiral handles and geometric patterns, reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery. Its surface is matte and aged, with dark brown motifs and a wide, flared rim.
description_view_0: Front view showing both spiral handles, geometric patterns, and central motifs clearly visible on the body.
description_view_1: Side view with one handle directly facing forward, obscuring part of the rim and body, emphasizing the handle's thickness.
description_view_2: Similar to the front, both spiral handles are visible, but the lighting and angle highlight the curvature and depth of the patterns.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the other handle facing forward, mirroring view 1 and showing the handle's robust structure.
